question: Who made the world?
answer: God made the world.

question: Who is God?
answer: God is the Creator of all things; He is a spirit, without a body, who has always existed, and who is infinitely perfect.

question: Is it necessary for us to know God?
answer: Yes; it is necessary for us to know God, because this knowledge teaches us to love and serve Him, and through this we obtain happiness both in this life and in the next.

question: Of what must we know about God?
answer: We must know: (1) That there is one God; (2) That there are three Persons in one God; (3) That the three Persons are the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost; (4) That the Son became man and died for us; (5) That the Holy Ghost comes down to us to give us grace and to teach us.

question: Is there only one God?
answer: Yes; there is only one God.

question: Are there three Persons in one God?
answer: Yes; there are three Persons in one God — the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost.

question: Are the three Persons all God?
answer: Yes; the three Persons are all God — the Father is God, the Son is God, and the Holy Ghost is God.

question: Is there any difference between the three Persons?
answer: Yes; the three Persons differ from one another. The Father is not the Son, the Son is not the Holy Ghost, and the Holy Ghost is neither the Father nor the Son.

question: What do we mean by the Holy Trinity?
answer: By the Holy Trinity we mean that there are three Persons in one God, and that all three are distinct from one another while yet being but one God.

question: Did God make all things?
answer: Yes; God made all things.

question: How did God make all things?
answer: God made all things from nothing, by simply willing or desiring them.

question: Why did God make all things?
answer: God made all things for His own glory and for our benefit.

question: Who was the first man?
answer: Adam was the first man.

question: Were our first parents ever innocent?
answer: Yes; our first parents, Adam and Eve, were innocent when they were created.

question: Were our first parents ever tempted?
answer: Yes; our first parents were tempted by the devil.

question: What is the angel called who tempted our first parents?
answer: The angel who tempted our first parents is called Lucifer, or Satan.

question: Did our first parents resist the temptation?
answer: No; our first parents did not resist the temptation, but ate the forbidden fruit.

question: What is this sin called?
answer: This sin is called the sin of our first parents, or Original Sin.

question: What befell our first parents on account of their sin?
answer: On account of their sin our first parents lost the gift of sanctifying grace, became enemies of God, and fell under the power of the devil; and they were excluded from Heaven and condemned to suffer.

question: What else befell our first parents besides losing the gift of sanctifying grace?
answer: Besides losing the gift of sanctifying grace, our first parents became subject to death, to suffering, and to a darkness of understanding, and they had to earn their bread by the sweat of their brow.

question: Did any of our first parents' descendants share in their sin?
answer: Yes; all of our first parents' descendants share in their sin, because they were their children and were to come from them.

question: Were all men, then, born in sin?
answer: Yes; all men were born in sin, in the state of original sin.

question: Is there any way to free ourselves from the state of original sin?
answer: Yes; we can free ourselves from the state of original sin by receiving the sacrament of Baptism.

question: Who gave us the command to be baptized?
answer: Our Blessed Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ gave us the command to be baptized.

question: What are the words our Lord used when He commanded us to be baptized?
answer: Our Lord said: 'Go, teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost.' — Matt. xxviii, 19.

question: What is the reason our Lord became man?
answer: Our Lord became man to redeem us — that is, to buy us back from the slavery of sin and the devil, and to make satisfaction for our sins.

question: Is our Lord God?
answer: Yes; our Lord Jesus Christ is God.

question: Is our Lord man also?
answer: Yes; our Lord is both God and man.

question: Was the Son of God always man?
answer: No; the Son of God was not always man, but became man about two thousand years ago, when He was born of the Blessed Virgin Mary.

question: Is the Blessed Virgin Mary truly the Mother of God?
answer: Yes; the Blessed Virgin Mary is truly the Mother of God, because she gave flesh to the Son of God, who is also the Son of Mary.

question: What do we mean when we say that Jesus Christ is the only-begotten Son of God?
answer: When we say that Jesus Christ is the only-begotten Son of God we mean that He is the natural and proper Son of God, and that He is of the same substance with the Father.

question: Where was Jesus Christ born?
answer: Jesus Christ was born in a stable at Bethlehem, a small town in Judea.

question: When was Jesus Christ born?
answer: Jesus Christ was born about two thousand years ago, on Christmas Day.

question: Who were the godparents of our Lord?
answer: The godparents of our Lord were St. Joseph and the Blessed Virgin Mary.

question: What is the name of the place where our Lord usually lived?
answer: The name of the place where our Lord usually lived is Nazareth.

question: Did our Lord work while He lived at Nazareth?
answer: Yes; our Lord worked at Nazareth, following the trade of a carpenter.

question: What did our Lord do after He left Nazareth?
answer: After He left Nazareth our Lord began to preach and teach.

question: Where did our Lord preach and teach?
answer: Our Lord preached and taught in the synagogues, in the streets, in the fields, and especially on a mountain near the Lake of Galilee, which is called the Mount of the Beatitudes.

question: What is the name of the sermon our Lord gave on the mountain?
answer: The name of this sermon is the 'Sermon on the Mount.' It contains the eight Beatitudes.

question: What are the Beatitudes?
answer: The Beatitudes are the eight blessings our Lord pronounced on those who practise the virtues there mentioned.

question: Who were the persons that heard our Lord preach?
answer: The persons that heard our Lord were the multitudes — the common people — and especially His Apostles and disciples.

question: Who are the Apostles?
answer: The Apostles are the twelve disciples whom our Lord chose to be His special companions and teachers.

question: Who was the chief of the Apostles?
answer: St. Peter was the chief of the Apostles.

question: What did our Lord do to show that He was the Son of God?
answer: Our Lord showed He was the Son of God by working many miracles.

question: What is a miracle?
answer: A miracle is a wonderful effect produced by the power of God, which surpasses the power of any created being.

question: What did our Lord do at the Last Supper?
answer: At the Last Supper our Lord instituted the Holy Eucharist, and then He washed the feet of His Apostles.

question: What is the Holy Eucharist?
answer: The Holy Eucharist is the true Body and Blood of Jesus Christ, together with His soul and divinity, under the appearances of bread and wine.

question: How did our Lord suffer?
answer: Our Lord suffered by being betrayed, by being falsely accused, by being condemned to death, by being mocked, by being scourged, and by being crucified.

question: Who betrayed our Lord?
answer: Judas Iscariot betrayed our Lord.

question: Who condemned our Lord to death?
answer: Pontius Pilate, the Roman governor, condemned our Lord to death.

question: Where did our Lord die?
answer: Our Lord died on Mount Calvary, outside the city of Jerusalem.

question: When did our Lord die?
answer: Our Lord died on Good Friday, at three o'clock in the afternoon.

question: What happened when our Lord died?
answer: When our Lord died the veil of the temple was rent, the earth quaked, the dead came forth from their graves, and the centurion declared: 'Indeed this was the Son of God.'

question: Who buried our Lord?
answer: Joseph of Arimathea and Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews, buried our Lord.

question: What happened on Easter Sunday?
answer: On Easter Sunday our Lord rose from the dead.

question: How long did our Lord remain on earth after His Resurrection?
answer: Our Lord remained on earth forty days after His Resurrection.

question: Where did our Lord go when He left the earth?
answer: When our Lord left the earth He ascended into Heaven.

question: When did our Lord ascend into Heaven?
answer: Our Lord ascended into Heaven on Ascension Day, forty days after Easter.

question: Where is our Lord now?
answer: Our Lord is now in Heaven, seated at the right hand of God the Father.

question: What did our Lord promise to send us?
answer: Our Lord promised to send us the Holy Ghost.

question: When did the Holy Ghost come down upon the Apostles?
answer: The Holy Ghost came down upon the Apostles on Pentecost Sunday, ten days after our Lord ascended into Heaven.

question: What is the grace of God?
answer: The grace of God is a supernatural gift of God, bestowed on us through the merits of Jesus Christ, for our salvation.

question: How many kinds of grace are there?
answer: There are two kinds of grace: sanctifying grace and actual grace.

question: What is sanctifying grace?
answer: Sanctifying grace is that grace which makes us holy and pleasing to God, and which gives us a right to Heaven.

question: What is actual grace?
answer: Actual grace is that grace which helps us to do good and to avoid evil.

question: What do we call those who have sanctifying grace in their souls?
answer: Those who have sanctifying grace in their souls are called the friends of God, or 'just souls.'

question: Is sanctifying grace lost by every mortal sin?
answer: Yes; sanctifying grace is lost by every mortal sin.

question: What must we do to recover sanctifying grace when we have lost it?
answer: To recover sanctifying grace we must go to Confession.

question: Is actual grace also lost by mortal sin?
answer: Yes; actual grace is also lost by mortal sin, because the soul that is deprived of sanctifying grace is also deprived of the power of doing good.

question: Can we earn actual grace?
answer: Yes; we can earn actual grace by prayer and by doing the will of God.

question: Is sanctifying grace also given to us as a reward for our good works?
answer: Yes; sanctifying grace is also given to us as a reward for our good works, if we are in the state of grace.

question: Are we able to do any good without the help of actual grace?
answer: No; we are not able to do any good without the help of actual grace.

question: Will God refuse us actual grace if we ask for it?
answer: No; God will not refuse us actual grace if we ask for it, because He has promised to give it to those who ask.

question: What is the greatest gift God gave us?
answer: The greatest gift God gave us is His only-begotten Son, our Lord Jesus Christ.

question: How did God give us His only-begotten Son?
answer: God gave us His only-begotten Son by making Him become man and die for us.

question: Why did God give His only-begotten Son to die for us?
answer: God gave His only-begotten Son to die for us because He loved us, and because there was no other way to save us.

question: What do we call the gift of God in sending His Son to die for us?
answer: We call it the Mystery of the Incarnation — that is, the mystery of God becoming man.

question: What does the word 'Christian' mean?
answer: The word 'Christian' means a follower of Christ.

question: What must we do to be true Christians?
answer: To be true Christians we must believe in our Lord Jesus Christ, keep His commandments, and receive the sacraments.

question: Did our Lord make any promise to those who would believe in Him?
answer: Yes; our Lord promised that whoever believes in Him shall have eternal life.

question: Will those who do not believe be saved?
answer: No; those who do not believe cannot be saved, because faith is necessary for salvation.

question: How may we know the true Church of Christ?
answer: We may know the true Church of Christ because it teaches the doctrine of Christ, keeps the sacraments Christ instituted, and has the mark of unity.

question: What do you mean by the unity of the Church?
answer: By the unity of the Church I mean that all the members of the Church should be one — one flock under one shepherd.

question: Is the Catholic Church the true Church of Christ?
answer: Yes; the Catholic Church is the true Church of Christ.

question: What are the marks of the Catholic Church?
answer: The marks of the Catholic Church are: one, holy, Catholic, and apostolic.

question: Who is the head of the Catholic Church?
answer: The Pope, or Bishop of Rome, is the head of the Catholic Church.

question: Who is the head of the Church after the Pope?
answer: After the Pope, the head of the Church is the bishop of the diocese.

question: What do we mean when we say the Church is infallible?
answer: When we say the Church is infallible we mean that she cannot err in teaching the doctrine of Christ.

question: Can the Church err in anything else besides doctrine?
answer: No; the Church cannot err in anything besides doctrine, because our Lord promised to guide her in all matters of faith and morals.

question: Is it necessary for salvation to belong to the true Church?
answer: Yes; it is necessary for salvation to belong to the true Church, because our Lord said: 'He that believeth not shall be condemned.'

question: What are the chief commandments of God?
answer: The chief commandments of God are the ten Commandments of God.

question: What are the ten Commandments of God?
answer:
The ten Commandments of God are:
1. I am the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not have strange gods before Me.
2. Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain.
3. Remember thou keep holy the Sabbath day.
4. Honor thy father and thy mother.
5. Thou shalt not kill.
6. Thou shalt not commit adultery.
7. Thou shalt not steal.
8. Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbor.
9. Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's wife.
10. Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's goods.

question: Which is the first Commandment?
answer: I am the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not have strange gods before Me.

question: What does the first Commandment forbid?
answer: The first Commandment forbids the worship of false gods, and all sin against religion.

question: What are the sins against religion?
answer: The sins against religion are: apostasy, heresy, schism, and the worship of false gods.

question: What is apostasy?
answer: Apostasy is the total denial of the Christian faith.

question: What is heresy?
answer: Heresy is the willful denial of some truth taught by the Church, after one has accepted the Christian faith.

question: What is schism?
answer: Schism is the refusal to submit to the Pope, or the separation from the Church.

question: What is the worship of false gods?
answer: The worship of false gods is the giving to creatures the honor and worship that belong to God alone.

question: What is the second Commandment?
answer: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain.

question: What does the second Commandment forbid?
answer: The second Commandment forbids all false, careless, or irreverent use of God's name, and the names of the saints and sacred things.

question: What is the third Commandment?
answer: Remember thou keep holy the Sabbath day.

question: What does the third Commandment command?
answer: The third Commandment commands us to keep holy the Sunday, or the Lord's Day.

question: What must we do on Sunday?
answer: On Sunday we must hear Mass and avoid servile work.

question: What is servile work?
answer: Servile work is work that is done mainly with the body, or the labor of servants and hired persons.

question: What is the fourth Commandment?
answer: Honor thy father and thy mother.

question: What does the fourth Commandment command?
answer: The fourth Commandment commands us to love, honor, and obey our parents, and all who are placed in authority over us.

question: What does the fourth Commandment forbid?
answer: The fourth Commandment forbids us to dishonor, disobey, or neglect our parents and superiors.

question: What is the fifth Commandment?
answer: Thou shalt not kill.

question: What does the fifth Commandment forbid?
answer: The fifth Commandment forbids murder, dueling, suicide, and all unlawful violence.

question: What is the sixth Commandment?
answer: Thou shalt not commit adultery.

question: What does the sixth Commandment forbid?
answer: The sixth Commandment forbids all sins of impurity.

question: What is the seventh Commandment?
answer: Thou shalt not steal.

question: What does the seventh Commandment forbid?
answer: The seventh Commandment forbids theft, and all unjust taking or keeping of another's property.

question: What is the eighth Commandment?
answer: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbor.

question: What does the eighth Commandment forbid?
answer: The eighth Commandment forbids lying, detraction, calumny, and rash judgment.

question: What is the ninth Commandment?
answer: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's wife.

question: What does the ninth Commandment forbid?
answer: The ninth Commandment forbids all wicked desires for another's wife or husband.

question: What is the tenth Commandment?
answer: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's goods.

question: What does the tenth Commandment forbid?
answer: The tenth Commandment forbids all wicked desires for another's goods.

question: What are the two great Commandments on which all the law and the prophets depend?
answer: The two great Commandments are: 'Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with thy whole heart, and with thy whole soul, and with thy whole mind.' And: 'Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.'

question: What are the commandments of the Church?
answer: The commandments of the Church are chiefly six.

question: What are the six chief commandments of the Church?
answer:
The six chief commandments of the Church are:
1. To hear Mass on Sundays and holy days of obligation.
2. To fast on the days commanded.
3. To go to Confession at least once a year.
4. To receive Holy Communion at least once a year, namely from Ash Wednesday to Trinity Sunday.
5. To contribute to the support of the Church.
6. Not to marry Catholics with non-Catholics without the permission of the Church.

question: How many Precepts of the Church are there besides the chief ones?
answer: Besides the chief Precepts, there are several others, which bind under venial sin.

question: What are these other Precepts of the Church?
answer:
These other Precepts of the Church are:
1. To observe the feast days of obligation.
2. To abstain from meat on Fridays and on the vigils of certain feasts.
3. Not to practice usury or money-lending for interest.
4. To attend to the Christian education of one's children.

question: How many Precepts of the Church are there in all?
answer: There are ten Precepts of the Church in all.

question: Why does the Church command us to hear Mass on Sundays?
answer: The Church commands us to hear Mass on Sundays because the holy Sacrifice of the Mass is the most perfect act of worship we can offer to God.

question: How does the hearing of Mass fulfill the third Commandment?
answer: The hearing of Mass fulfills the third Commandment because it is the principal manner of keeping holy the Sunday.

question: Does the hearing of Mass on Sundays excuse us from the other duties of the day?
answer: No; the hearing of Mass does not excuse us from the other duties of the day, such as hearing sermons, instructing our children, etc.

question: What days are holy days of obligation in this country?
answer:
The holy days of obligation in this country are:
1. The Feast of the Immaculate Conception (December 8th).
2. The Feast of the Assumption (August 15th).
3. The Feast of All Saints (November 1st).
4. The Feast of the Ascension (forty days after Easter).
5. The Feast of the Nativity (December 25th).
6. The Feast of the Circumcision (January 1st).
7. The Feast of the Epiphany (January 6th).

question: What does the Church command us to fast on?
answer: The Church commands us to fast on the days of fasting and abstinence.

question: What are the days of fasting?
answer: The days of fasting are Ash Wednesday, Good Friday, and the vigils of certain feasts.

question: What is the law of abstinence?
answer: The law of abstinence forbids the use of meat and the juice of meat.

question: Who are bound to fast?
answer: All adults are bound to fast, unless lawfully excused.

question: Who are excused from fasting?
answer: Those are excused from fasting who are under age, or who are weak, or who have to work hard, etc.

question: Why does the Church command us to go to Confession at least once a year?
answer: The Church commands us to go to Confession at least once a year because we should be always prepared to make a good confession, and because we must confess our sins to the priest when we have mortal sin on our conscience.

question: Why does the Church command us to receive Holy Communion at least once a year?
answer: The Church commands us to receive Holy Communion at least once a year so that we may be strengthened by the Bread of Life and be prepared to die well.

question: What is the sixth Commandment of the Church?
answer: The sixth Commandment of the Church is: 'Not to marry Catholics with non-Catholics without the permission of the Church.'

question: What are the other four Precepts of the Church?
answer:
The other four Precepts of the Church are:
1. To observe the feast days of obligation.
2. To abstain from meat on Fridays and on the vigils of certain feasts.
3. Not to practice usury or money-lending for interest.
4. To attend to the Christian education of one's children.

question: How many days of fast are there?
answer: There are two days of fast: Ash Wednesday and Good Friday.

question: What days are vigils?
answer: Vigils are the evenings before certain feast days, and we are bound to fast on those days as well.

question: What is the use of the Commandments of the Church?
answer: The Commandments of the Church are useful to lead us to eternal life, because they are the means appointed by our Lord for our sanctification.

question: Does the keeping of the Commandments free us from the necessity of avoiding sin?
answer: No; the keeping of the Commandments does not free us from the necessity of avoiding sin, because we must keep ourselves free from all mortal and venial sin, and from all evil desires.

question: How do we sin?
answer: We sin by thought, word, deed, or omission.

question: What is a sin of thought?
answer: A sin of thought is any willful evil desire or thought in the mind.

question: What is a sin of word?
answer: A sin of word is any willful evil speaking or lying.

question: What is a sin of deed?
answer: A sin of deed is any willful evil action.

question: What is a sin of omission?
answer: A sin of omission is the willful neglect to do something commanded or required.

question: How many kinds of sin are there?
answer: There are two kinds of sin: mortal sin and venial sin.

question: What is mortal sin?
answer: Mortal sin is a grievous offense against God, and it destroys the life of the soul in us.

question: Why is it called mortal sin?
answer: It is called mortal sin because it kills the soul — that is, it destroys the life of sanctifying grace in the soul.

question: How many things are necessary to make a sin mortal?
answer:
Three things are necessary to make a sin mortal:
1. The thought, word, or deed must be a serious offense.
2. The person must know it is a serious offense.
3. The person must fully consent to it.

question: What is venial sin?
answer: Venial sin is a slight offense against God, or a violation of some lesser law.

question: Does venial sin destroy the life of grace in the soul?
answer: No; venial sin does not destroy the life of grace in the soul, but it weakens it and makes us less fit for the service of God.

question: What is the difference between mortal and venial sin?
answer:
The difference between mortal and venial sin is:
1. Mortal sin destroys the life of the soul; venial sin only weakens it.
2. Mortal sin makes us enemies of God; venial sin makes us less pleasing to Him.
3. Mortal sin excludes from Heaven; venial sin does not.

question: What are the effects of mortal sin?
answer:
The effects of mortal sin are:
1. The loss of sanctifying grace.
2. The loss of the right to Heaven.
3. The making of satisfaction to God for the offense committed.

question: Can we make satisfaction for our sins ourselves?
answer: Yes; we can make satisfaction for our sins ourselves, by doing penance and by suffering willingly the punishments which God sends upon us.

question: Is there any other way of making satisfaction for sin?
answer: Yes; our Lord Jesus Christ made satisfaction for all the sins of the world, and He merited for us the grace to make satisfaction ourselves.

question: How many kinds of satisfaction are there?
answer: There are three kinds of satisfaction: by suffering, by prayer, and by almsgiving.

question: What is the chief enemy of our souls?
answer: The chief enemy of our souls is the devil.

question: Does the devil tempt us to sin?
answer: Yes; the devil tempts us to sin in many ways.

question: What must we do to overcome the temptations of the devil?
answer: To overcome the temptations of the devil we must pray, resist, and fly from dangerous occasions.

question: What do we mean by flying from dangerous occasions?
answer: By flying from dangerous occasions we mean avoiding the persons, places, and things that are likely to lead us into sin.

question: What must we do to overcome our evil habits?
answer: To overcome our evil habits we must pray, do penance, and make use of the means of grace.

question: What is a sacrament?
answer: A sacrament is an outward sign instituted by Christ to give us grace.

question: How many sacraments are there?
answer: There are seven sacraments: Baptism, Confirmation, Holy Eucharist, Penance, Extreme Unction, Holy Orders, and Matrimony.

question: Do all the sacraments give grace?
answer: Yes; all the sacraments give grace, but only to those who receive them worthily.

question: Which are the sacraments that may be received only once?
answer: The sacraments that may be received only once are: Baptism, Confirmation, and Holy Orders.

question: Why may Baptism, Confirmation, and Holy Orders be received only once?
answer: These sacraments may be received only once because they give a character, or seal, that lasts forever.

question: What is the sacrament of Baptism?
answer: Baptism is a sacrament which cleanses us from original sin, and makes us children of God and members of the Church.

question: How is Baptism administered?
answer: Baptism is administered by pouring water on the head of the person to be baptized, while saying: 'I baptize thee in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost.'

question: Is Baptism necessary for salvation?
answer: Yes; Baptism is necessary for salvation, because our Lord said: 'Unless a man be born again of water and the Holy Ghost, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.'

question: What is the matter and form of Baptism?
answer: The matter of Baptism is water, and the form is: 'I baptize thee in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost.'

question: What is the sacrament of Confirmation?
answer: Confirmation is a sacrament by which we receive the Holy Ghost and are made strong and perfect Christians.

question: How is Confirmation administered?
answer: Confirmation is administered by the bishop, who anoints the forehead of the person with holy chrism, and says: 'I sign thee with the sign of the Cross, and confirm thee with the chrism of salvation, in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost.'

question: What is the matter and form of Confirmation?
answer: The matter of Confirmation is holy chrism, and the form is the words the bishop says in administering it.

question: What is the sacrament of the Holy Eucharist?
answer: The Holy Eucharist is a sacrament which contains the true Body and Blood of Jesus Christ, together with His soul and divinity, under the appearances of bread and wine.

question: What is the change called which takes place in the bread and wine at the words of consecration?
answer: The change is called Transubstantiation.

question: What is Transubstantiation?
answer: Transubstantiation is the change of the whole substance of the bread and wine into the Body and Blood of our Lord.

question: What is the matter of the Holy Eucharist?
answer: The matter of the Holy Eucharist is wheaten bread and wine made from grapes.

question: What must we do to receive Holy Communion worthily?
answer: To receive Holy Communion worthily we must be in the state of grace, and have fasted from midnight.

question: What is the sacrament of Penance?
answer: Penance is a sacrament by which the sins committed after Baptism are forgiven.

question: What are the parts of the sacrament of Penance?
answer: The parts of the sacrament of Penance are: contrition, confession, satisfaction, and absolution.

question: What is contrition?
answer: Contrition is a hearty sorrow for our sins, and a firm purpose never to sin again.

question: What is confession?
answer: Confession is the telling of our sins to a priest who has power to forgive them.

question: What is the power of the priest in confession?
answer: The priest has the power to forgive or retain sins, which power was given to him by our Lord when He said: 'Whose sins you shall forgive, they are forgiven them; and whose sins you shall retain, they are retained.'

question: What is satisfaction?
answer: Satisfaction is the penance which the priest gives us to perform.

question: What is absolution?
answer: Absolution is the forgiveness which the priest grants us when we have made a good confession.

question: What must we do before going to Confession?
answer: Before going to Confession we must examine our conscience, and be sorry for our sins, and firmly resolve not to commit them again.

question: How should we make our confession?
answer: We should make our confession in a clear and distinct manner, telling the kind of sin and the number of times it was committed.

question: What is the sacrament of Extreme Unction?
answer: Extreme Unction is a sacrament which gives grace to souls that are in danger of death.

question: How is Extreme Unction administered?
answer: Extreme Unction is administered by anointing the sick person on the eyes, ears, nostrils, lips, hands, and feet, with holy oil, while saying the appointed prayers.

question: What is the sacrament of Holy Orders?
answer: Holy Orders is a sacrament by which bishops, priests, and other ministers of the Church are ordained.

question: What are the three degrees of Holy Orders?
answer: The three degrees of Holy Orders are: bishops, priests, and deacons.

question: What is the sacrament of Matrimony?
answer: Matrimony is a sacrament which unites a man and a woman as husband and wife, and gives them grace to live together faithfully.

question: What is necessary for the validity of Matrimony?
answer: For the validity of Matrimony it is necessary that the parties be free to marry, that they give their consent, and that the consent be witnessed by a priest or deacon and two witnesses.

question: What are the Sacramentals?
answer: The Sacramentals are holy things, actions, or words which the Church uses to obtain for us spiritual favors.

question: Do the Sacramentals give grace?
answer: The Sacramentals do not give grace of themselves, but they dispose us to receive grace.

question: What are the chief Sacramentals?
answer: The chief Sacramentals are: holy water, holy oils, blessed candles, blessed ashes, crucifixes, medals, rosaries, scapulars, and the Sign of the Cross.

question: What is the Sign of the Cross?
answer: The Sign of the Cross is a blessing made by touching the forehead, breast, and shoulders, while saying: 'In the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost. Amen.'

question: What is holy water?
answer: Holy water is water blessed by the priest with solemn ceremonies.

question: What is a blessing?
answer: A blessing is the asking of God's favor for persons or things.

question: Who can give the blessing?
answer: Bishops and priests can give the blessing.

question: What is a consecration?
answer: A consecration is a blessing by which persons or things are set apart for the service of God.

question: What things are consecrated?
answer: Altars, chalices, patens, church buildings, and persons dedicated to God are consecrated.

question: What is a medal?
answer: A medal is a piece of metal stamped with the image of some saint or mystery, and blessed by the Church.

question: What is a scapular?
answer: A scapular is a part of the habit of some religious orders, consisting of two small pieces of cloth joined together by strings.

question: What is the rosary?
answer: The rosary is a string of beads used for counting the prayers we say in honor of the Blessed Virgin Mary.

question: How many decades are there in the rosary?
answer: There are fifteen decades in the rosary, each consisting of one Our Father and ten Hail Marys.

question: What are the mysteries of the rosary?
answer: The mysteries of the rosary are the joyful, sorrowful, and glorious mysteries, which recall to us the chief events in the life of our Lord and His Blessed Mother.

question: What is prayer?
answer: Prayer is the raising of our minds and hearts to God to adore Him, to thank Him, and to ask Him for what we need.

question: How many kinds of prayer are there?
answer: There are three kinds of prayer: vocal, mental, and contemplation.

question: What is vocal prayer?
answer: Vocal prayer is prayer in which we use words, either spoken or sung.

question: What is mental prayer?
answer: Mental prayer is prayer in which we think deeply of God and divine things without using words.

question: What is the best prayer?
answer: The best prayer is the prayer that comes from the heart.

question: What are the four conditions necessary for good prayer?
answer: The four conditions necessary for good prayer are: (1) we must pray with attention; (2) we must pray with humility; (3) we must pray with confidence; (4) we must pray with perseverance.

question: What is attention in prayer?
answer: Attention in prayer is the keeping of our minds from wandering, so that we may be united with God in spirit while we pray.

question: What is humility in prayer?
answer: Humility in prayer is the realization of our own unworthiness to ask God for anything.

question: What is confidence in prayer?
answer: Confidence in prayer is the firm belief that God will hear us, because He has promised to do so.

question: What is perseverance in prayer?
answer: Perseverance in prayer is the continuing to pray until our prayer is answered.

question: What is the Our Father?
answer: The Our Father is the prayer taught by our Lord to His disciples.

question: What are the parts of the Our Father?
answer: The parts of the Our Father are: the introduction, the first three petitions, the last four petitions, and the conclusion.

question: What is the introduction of the Our Father?
answer: The introduction is: 'Our Father who art in Heaven.'

question: What do we ask in the first three petitions?
answer: In the first three petitions we ask: (1) that God's name may be honored; (2) that His kingdom may come; (3) that His will may be done.

question: What do we ask in the last four petitions?
answer: In the last four petitions we ask: (1) for our daily bread; (2) for the forgiveness of our sins; (3) that we may be delivered from evil; (4) that all these favors may be obtained.

question: What is the conclusion of the Our Father?
answer: The conclusion is: 'For thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, forever. Amen.'

question: What is the Hail Mary?
answer: The Hail Mary is a prayer in honor of the Blessed Virgin Mary.

question: What are the parts of the Hail Mary?
answer: The parts of the Hail Mary are: the angel's greeting, Elizabeth's greeting, and the closing petition.

question: What is the angel's greeting?
answer: The angel's greeting is: 'Hail, Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee.'

question: What is Elizabeth's greeting?
answer: Elizabeth's greeting is: 'Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.'

question: What is the closing petition of the Hail Mary?
answer: The closing petition is: 'Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us sinners now, and at the hour of our death. Amen.'

question: What is the Glory Be?
answer: The Glory Be is a prayer in honor of the Holy Trinity.

question: What are the prayers before Mass?
answer: The prayers before Mass are the prayers at the foot of the altar, the Introit, the Kyrie, the Gloria, and the Collect.

question: What are the prayers during Mass?
answer: The prayers during Mass are the Epistle, the Gospel, the Creed, the Offertory, the Secret, the Preface, the Canon, the Pater Noster, the Agnus Dei, and the Communion.

question: What are the prayers after Mass?
answer: The prayers after Mass are the Postcommunion, the Ite Missa Est, and the last Gospel.

question: What is the reason for which our Lord instituted the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass?
answer: Our Lord instituted the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass to continue His sacrifice on the Cross, and to apply its fruits to our souls.

question: What is the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass?
answer: The Holy Sacrifice of the Mass is the sacrifice of the Body and Blood of our Lord Jesus Christ, under the appearances of bread and wine.

question: How many parts are there in the Mass?
answer: There are four parts in the Mass: the Preparation, the Liturgy of the Word, the Liturgy of the Eucharist, and the Communion.

question: What is the Preparation at Mass?
answer: The Preparation at Mass consists of the prayers at the foot of the altar, the Introit, the Kyrie Eleison, the Gloria, and the Collect.

question: What is the Liturgy of the Word at Mass?
answer: The Liturgy of the Word at Mass consists of the Epistle, the Gradual, the Alleluia or Tract, the Gospel, the Credo, and the Offertory.

question: What is the Liturgy of the Eucharist at Mass?
answer: The Liturgy of the Eucharist at Mass consists of the Offertory, the Secret, the Preface, the Canon, the Pater Noster, and the Agnus Dei.

question: What is the Communion at Mass?
answer: The Communion at Mass consists of the priest's Communion, the people's Communion, the Postcommunion, the Ite Missa Est, and the last Gospel.

question: What is the Offertory?
answer: The Offertory is the part of the Mass in which the priest offers the bread and wine to God.

question: What is the Canon of the Mass?
answer: The Canon of the Mass is the unchangeable part of the Mass which begins with the Te Igitam and ends with the Per Ipsum.

question: What is the Consecration?
answer: The Consecration is the part of the Mass in which the priest, by the words of our Lord, changes the bread and wine into the Body and Blood of our Lord.

question: What is the matter of the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass?
answer: The matter of the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass is bread made from wheat and wine made from grapes.

question: How does the priest change the bread and wine into the Body and Blood of our Lord?
answer: The priest changes the bread and wine by the power which he received in his ordination, and by the words of consecration which our Lord spoke at the Last Supper.

question: What must we do at Mass?
answer: At Mass we must hear the prayers with attention, join in the prayers as much as we can, and offer ourselves with the priest.

question: What is a Low Mass?
answer: A Low Mass is a Mass said without singing.

question: What is a High Mass?
answer: A High Mass is a Mass sung by the priest with the help of a choir.

question: What is a Solemn Mass?
answer: A Solemn Mass is a High Mass celebrated with incense, lights, and deacon and subdeacon.

question: What is the virtue of faith?
answer: The virtue of faith is a supernatural gift of God which enables us to believe whatever God has revealed.

question: What does the virtue of faith help us to do?
answer: The virtue of faith helps us to know that God has spoken, and to believe whatever He has said.

question: What is the virtue of hope?
answer: The virtue of hope is a supernatural gift of God which enables us to trust that we shall obtain what God has promised.

question: What does the virtue of hope help us to do?
answer: The virtue of hope helps us to trust in God, and to expect from Him the means necessary to save our souls.

question: What is the virtue of charity?
answer: The virtue of charity is a supernatural gift of God which enables us to love God above all things, and our neighbor as ourselves.

question: What does the virtue of charity help us to do?
answer: The virtue of charity helps us to love God and our neighbor, and to do good to all.

question: What are the theological virtues?
answer: The theological virtues are faith, hope, and charity.

question: Why are they called theological virtues?
answer: They are called theological virtues because they have God for their object.

question: What are the cardinal virtues?
answer: The cardinal virtues are prudence, justice, fortitude, and temperance.

question: What is prudence?
answer: Prudence is the virtue by which we govern our actions aright.

question: What is justice?
answer: Justice is the virtue by which we give to everyone what is his due.

question: What is fortitude?
answer: Fortitude is the virtue by which we are strengthened to do good and to resist evil.

question: What is temperance?
answer: Temperance is the virtue by which we control our desires and use the goods of this world in moderation.

question: What are the gifts of the Holy Ghost?
answer: The gifts of the Holy Ghost are: wisdom, understanding, counsel, fortitude, knowledge, piety, and fear of the Lord.

question: What are the fruits of the Holy Ghost?
answer: The fruits of the Holy Ghost are: charity, joy, peace, patience, benignity, goodness, longanimity, mildness, faith, modesty, continency, and chastity.

question: What is the last thing that will happen to everyone?
answer: The last thing that will happen to everyone is death.

question: What is death?
answer: Death is the separation of the soul from the body.

question: What will happen to the body after death?
answer: After death the body will return to the earth, but it will be raised up again at the end of the world.

question: What will happen to the soul after death?
answer: After death the soul will go either to Heaven, to Purgatory, or to Hell.

question: What is Purgatory?
answer: Purgatory is a place where souls are purified from their sins before they enter Heaven.

question: Who go to Purgatory?
answer: Those go to Purgatory who die in the state of grace but have not satisfied God's justice for their sins.

question: What is Hell?
answer: Hell is a place of eternal punishment for the damned.

question: Who go to Hell?
answer: Those go to Hell who die in mortal sin.

question: What is the punishment of Hell?
answer: The punishment of Hell is eternal separation from God, and endless suffering.

question: What is Heaven?
answer: Heaven is the place of eternal happiness where the souls of the just enjoy the vision of God.

question: Who go to Heaven?
answer: Those go to Heaven who die in the state of grace and have no sin to be punished for.

question: What is the reward of Heaven?
answer: The reward of Heaven is the eternal vision and possession of God, and everlasting happiness.

question: What is the end of the world?
answer: The end of the world is the time when God will judge all men.

question: What will happen at the end of the world?
answer: At the end of the world our Lord will come to judge all men, and the bodies of the dead will rise from the earth.

question: What is the Last Judgment?
answer: The Last Judgment is the judgment of all men by our Lord Jesus Christ at the end of the world.

question: Will the judgment of the Last Day be public?
answer: Yes; the judgment of the Last Day will be public, for all men will be judged in the presence of the whole world.

question: Will the judgment be the same for all?
answer: No; the judgment will not be the same for all. Some will be judged more strictly, and some more mildly.

question: Why will some be judged more strictly than others?
answer: Some will be judged more strictly because they had more knowledge and more opportunities to do good.

question: What are the four last things?
answer: The four last things are: Death, Judgment, Hell, and Heaven.

question: Why should we think often of the four last things?
answer: We should think often of the four last things because they help us to avoid sin and to do good.
